ace: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Acehnese.
aced: verb. simple past and past participle of ace
acne: noun. A skin condition, usually of the face, that is common in adolescents.
acned: adjective. Marked by acne; suffering from acne.
add: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Dzodinka.
advanced: verb. simple past and past participle of advance
ana: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Andaqui.
and: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Ansus.
ane: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Xârâcùù.
ava: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Avar.
ave: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Avestan.
cad: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Caddo.
cade: adjective. abandoned by its mother and reared by hand
cave: noun. A large, naturally-occurring cavity formed underground or in the face of a cliff or a hillside.
dace: noun. A shoal -forming fish of species Leuciscus leuciscus, common to swift rivers in England and Wales and in Europe.
dad: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Marik.
dance: noun. A sequence of rhythmic steps or movements usually performed to music, for pleasure or as a form of social interaction.
danced: verb. simple past and past participle of dance
daven: verb. to recite the Jewish liturgy; to pray
dean: noun. A senior official in a college or university, who may be in charge of a division or faculty (for example, the dean of science) or
den: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Slavey. (macrolanguage)
dev: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Domung.
deva: noun. A god in Vedic mythology, Hinduism and Buddhism.
nae: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Naka'ela.
vac: noun. Clipping of vacation (“extended period of time away from work or school”).
van: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Valman.
vanda: noun. Any orchid of the genus Vanda.
vaned: adjective. Having a vane or vanes.
vend: verb. Synonym of sell, to sell through a vending machine.
